title: lappula ceratophora
intro: Lappula ceratophora, also known as the 'prickly gromwell', is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The medicinal properties of Lappula ceratophora have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can provide health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Lappula ceratophora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
